overrides.override_summary: General release upon renewal or transfer may be limited. CA Corporations Code §31512 voids waivers of Franchise Investment Law rights. §20010 voids waivers under Franchise Relations Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee cannot be required to waive claims under CA franchise law as a condition of renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Massachusetts choice-of-law provision may not be enforceable under California law.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to apply CA law, which has stronger franchisee protections.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be free to operate competing businesses after termination/expiration.

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671.
overrides.why_it_matters: The $250/day prohibited product fee may not be enforceable in CA.

overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate in CA is 10% annually.
overrides.why_it_matters: The FA's 1.5%/month (18% annualized) interest rate is capped at 10% for CA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy filing alone may not be grounds for FA termination.

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law, including fraud in inducement, or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements. Anti-disclaimer provisions per §31512.1.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee from being pressured into waiving rights at signing.

overrides.override_summary: No statement or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements.
overrides.why_it_matters: Same protection as CA — franchisee signing documents cannot waive statutory rights.

overrides.override_summary: A provision requiring arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be compelled to arbitrate in Massachusetts; dispute resolution must be available in MI.

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause. Franchisee must receive written notice and 30 days to cure.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI law provides a floor of good cause and cure period that the FA cannot override.

overrides.override_summary: Not explicitly voided, but Michigan franchise law restricts franchisor's ability to refuse transfer without good cause and limits forced resale of non-unique items.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees may have stronger protections against unfair transfer refusals.

overrides.override_summary: A requirement that franchisee assent to a release that deprives franchisee of rights under the MI Franchise Investment Law is void. Post-relationship settlement is allowed.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release required at renewal/transfer cannot waive MI franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot refuse to renew without fairly compensating franchisee for FMV of inventory, equipment, etc. (if term < 5 years, or franchisee prohibited from similar business, or < 6 months notice of non-renewal).
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides financial protection at non-renewal. Less directly applicable since initial term is 10 years.

overrides.override_summary: MN Statutes §80C.21 and Rule 2860.4400J prohibit requiring litigation outside MN, waiver of jury trial, or requiring waiver of MN franchise law rights.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees cannot be forced to arbitrate/litigate in Massachusetts.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure required (Minn. Stat. §80C.14 subd. 3).
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer cure period than FA's 30 days; gives MN franchisees more time to address defaults.

overrides.override_summary: 180 days' notice of non-renewal required (Minn. Stat. §80C.14 subd. 4).
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice for non-renewal than any FA provision.

overrides.override_summary: Rule 2860.4400D prohibits requiring franchisee to assent to general release that relieves liability under MN franchise law.
overrides.why_it_matters: General release at renewal/transfer cannot waive MN statutory claims.

overrides.override_summary: No statement or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Same anti-waiver protection as CA and HI.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination, cancellation, or substantial change in competitive circumstances. 60 days to cure. 10 days to cure nonpayment.
overrides.why_it_matters: Substantial notice and cure protections under WI Fair Dealership Law.

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Law (Ch. 135) supersedes inconsistent FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: WI franchisees have the protections of the Fair Dealership Law regardless of FA choice-of-law.
